items.title: The Socceroos' greatest ever World Cup gamble just paid off

children.content: With his first World Cup team selection, Tony Popovic produced one of the biggest shocks in Socceroos' history. After 90 minutes against Türkiye, his gamble was vindicated.

articles.title: Socceroos quick hits: Irankunda's stunning strike and Beach's brilliance

children.content: Nestory Irankunda scored a goal for the ages and then celebrated like Tim Cahill, while Patrick Beach's palm proved enormous. Here are the five quick hits from the Socceroos vs Türkiye.

image.altText: Beach Irankunda Popvic composite

articles.title: Jubilant fans pack Federation Square to celebrate Socceroos win

children.content: In Melbourne, Federation Square became the epicentre of fan festivities, with capacity crowds packing into the CBD venue for a Socceroos viewing party.

image.altText: A Socceroo fan shows her allegiance with face paint

articles.title: Socceroos ecstasy as wondergoals deliver 2-0 World Cup victory over Türkiye

children.content: The Socceroos have claimed an incredible 2-0 win over Türkiye in their first World Cup group match thanks to sensational goals from Nestory Irankunda and Connor Metcalfe.

image.altText: Connor Metcalfe celebrates the his Socceroos teammates.

items.title: The obscure laws Trump is using to reshape Washington in his image

children.content: Project by project, Donald Trump is altering the United States capital, and in some cases, disrupting views and design that date back more than two centuries.

items.title: Hamilton claims famous Ferrari win, F1 leader suffers late car failure

children.content: Lewis Hamilton has wound back the clock and announced himself as a world championship contender again, claiming his maiden grand prix win as a Ferrari driver.

items.title: No Medicare, no family, no visa — these are the babies Australia doesn't count

children.content: For the Pacific Islander women who harvest the fruit and vegetables that stock Australia’s supermarket shelves, motherhood here can mean injury, debt, secrecy and fear.

items.title: Paddleboarder says shark attack victim he helped save was 'very unlucky'

articles.title: 'Nothing off the table': Coogee drone ban lifted, shark culls not ruled out

items.title: Ryan kicks six as Saints get home in GWS thriller at Docklands

items.title: Police officer in custody after Perth girl shot dead overseas

items.title: Fair Work rejects gas giant's claim strikes would harm the economy

children.content: One of Australia's biggest gas producers has lost a bid to stop industrial action at its Darwin facilities after claiming strikes would force shutdowns and cause significant damage to the nation's economy.

image.altText: Evening / dusk shot of Darwin harbour, industrial site, with yellow lights glowing and cranes in view.

items.title: Thousands rally in Belfast to condemn anti-immigrant rioting

children.content: The anti-racism rally came after nights of fiery violence in parts of Northern Ireland following the arrest of a 30-year-old man from Sudan on charges of attempted murder in a stabbing that left a man partly blind.

image.altText: A large crowd holds up anti-racism signs in the middle of a city.

items.title: Love story: When these childhood friends reunited, the spark was undeniable

children.content: It was a case of puppy love when Cambell first set eyes on Tiana at a Gold Coast housing complex when they were both nine years old. They lost touch when they entered their teens but reunited as adults and have been inseparable since.

items.title: The feminist and the bra fitter: Inside the world of Hannah Ferguson and her mum

items.title: Some think Britain's 'broken', and they are looking to Australia for inspiration
